instruction: In Terminal, check whether the tmux session "tmux-wait-check-b1" is waiting for input, and save the result as JSON to "$HOME/Desktop/tmux_result_2_1.json". Set "session" to "tmux-wait-check-b1" and set "waiting_for_input" to true if it is waiting for input, otherwise false.

command:
tmux new-session -d -s "tmux-wait-check-b1" "printf '%s
' 'Waiting for confirmation' 'Type y to continue' 'TMUX_WAITING_MARKER_2A'; read answer; if [ "$answer" = 'y' ]; then printf '%s
' 'TMUX_CONFIRMED_MARKER_2A'; else printf '%s
' 'TMUX_REJECTED_MARKER_2A'; fi; tail -f /dev/null"

reference_command:
python3 - <<'PY' > "$HOME/Desktop/tmux_result_2_1.json"
import json
import subprocess
out = subprocess.check_output(['tmux', 'capture-pane', '-t', 'tmux-wait-check-b1', '-p'], text=True)
waiting = 'Type y to continue' in out or 'Waiting for confirmation' in out
print(json.dumps({'session': 'tmux-wait-check-b1', 'waiting_for_input': waiting}))
PY
